summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, including bundling the server inside the app for a single-install experience.
summary.outcome: Created GitHub Actions release workflow, electron-builder config, and Homebrew Cask formula. Server bundling into the Electron app was implemented via extraResources and a CJS launcher wrapper. The Cask file was copied into a new homebrew-gossamer tap repo.

repo: The project has two separate package.json files: one at the root for the Node.js server (gossamer-serve) and one in app/ for the Electron frontend.
The server uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT for Prisma binary paths, making the bundled directory layout critical to preserve.
prisma was in devDependencies but is needed at runtime for pushSchema calls — moved to production dependencies.
Native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) require rebuilding against Electron's ABI using @electron/rebuild before packaging.
The app's main process gates server startup on absence of ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL, which is only set in dev mode.

code.finding: CJS wrapper created to bridge utilityProcess.fork() (which requires CJS) to the ESM server entry via dynamic import()
Modified to spawn the bundled server as a utilityProcess in production builds, pointing to the extraResources server/ directory
Added electron-builder config with extraResources to bundle server dist/, node_modules/, prisma/, and the CJS launcher from the root package

workflow: utilityProcess.fork() in Electron only accepts CJS entry points, requiring a wrapper when the target module is ESM.
Homebrew Casks must live in a Casks/ subdirectory within the tap repository.
GitHub Actions can automate DMG builds and release asset attachment on v* tag pushes using electron-builder.
Unsigned macOS apps built without an Apple Developer cert require users to manually approve via System Settings → Privacy & Security.

friction: The split repo structure (root server + app/ Electron) required extraResources to use ../ relative paths to reach the server build artifacts, making the config fragile.
prisma being in devDependencies was a hidden runtime dependency that would have caused failures after npm prune --production in CI.
The app is unsigned, which will cause macOS Gatekeeper to block first-run for all users without an Apple Developer account.
open_items: Consolidating root server code and app/ Electron code into a single package was discussed but explicitly deferred — would eliminate the ../ extraResources paths and dual build coordination.
SHA256 hashes in Casks/gossamer.rb are placeholders (REPLACE_WITH_*) — must be updated after the first real GitHub Release is published.
Apple code signing not set up — requires Apple Developer account ($99/yr) and adding signing secrets to the GitHub Actions workflow.
The gossamer-serve CLI binary entry point could remain in package.json to support headless use even after any future consolidation.
